name: Pane Carasau
method: Add yeast to one cup of warm water and let rest until bubbles form. In the meantime, mix the flours together and salt. When the yeast is proofed add water slowly to the flour mixture. You want dough that you can handle easily, but you don't want it too stiff. Add more flour, or more water, if needed. Knead dough for a few minutes to form a smooth ball. Let rise covered in a warm place for one hour. Punch down and knead for a few minutes, cover again, and let rise an additional hour.

Prepare baking sheets with parchment paper. When dough has risen for the second time, punch it down. On a semolina floured surface roll out dough very thin like a cracker. Cover with olive oil, and the seasoning that you would like to use, rosemary is another great option, pictured is coarse salt and a little cracked pepper. Transfer carefully to a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake at 400°F on middle rack until evenly browned. Remove from oven and let cool. When cooled completely crack the dough into rough uneven pieces, as pictured. Serving suggestion: Eat them like crackers with cheese and wine, or you can have it beside salad, it's a great substitute for bread. Serves 2-3. Olé
